strengths: Three specific, well-contextualized objects are identified with dates, sources and clear real-world contexts.
The student explicitly frames the exhibition around the prescribed IA prompt, demonstrating focus.
Appropriate academic practice is evident in the use of formatted citations and a structured bibliography.
weaknesses: Links between objects and the prompt are uneven: one commentary slips into moral judgment rather than exploring epistemic ownership.
Analysis often relies on unsupported assertions and lacks explicit TOK terminology (e.g. shared vs personal knowledge).
Sections end abruptly or mid-sentence, indicating incomplete development of key arguments.
The concluding synthesis is generic and fails to tie together how each object uniquely illustrates knowledge confinement.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition clearly meets the criteria for band 5â6: three specific objects are presented with real-world context and justified links to the IA prompt, supported by citations. However, uneven quality of analysis, reliance on assertion over sustained TOK argumentation, absence of key terminology, and incomplete passages limit the depth and precision of the work. These features align with the lower end of the 5â6 band, justifying a final holistic score of 5.

annotations.comment: The abstract outlines secrecy strategies but should explicitly tie each strategy back to how knowledge is confined within a community.
The citation is fully formatted and demonstrates good academic practice.
The summary of the article remains descriptive without explicitly linking to how scientology knowledge belongs only to insiders.
The prompt is clearly stated, framing the central knowledge question effectively and anchoring the exhibition in the prescribed IA prompt.
The selected album cover provides tangible context, but the analysis should explain how the image itself communicates community-specific knowledge.
The object is precisely identified with title, artist and source, establishing a clear real-world context for the first example.
This description remains largely historical and descriptive rather than exploring the epistemic implications of sampling Holidayâs lyrics.
The argument shifts to moral judgement rather than analyzing who has rightful interpretive authority over the lyrics, weakening the TOK focus.
The analysis ends abruptly. Complete this thought and incorporate explicit TOK concepts such as shared vs. personal knowledge.
The continuation acknowledges community-specific understanding of trauma but needs an explicit statement on how this links to the prompt.
The title of Object 2 is clearly labeled with source details, establishing real-world context and credibility.
The description of OT-level secrecy is detailed and anchors the object in a clear real-world practice of knowledge gating.
This paragraph outlines exclusion but lacks TOK terminology around interpretative frameworks to deepen the analysis.
The Turkmenistan media guide is clearly presented with title and date, situating the object firmly in its context.
The photograph is relevant but the commentary should explain how this visual example demonstrates community-specific control of information.
The description of government monopoly is accurate, but the student should tie it explicitly to the prompt about belonging to particular communities.
This analysis notes censorship but should articulate how blocked knowledge shapes an exclusive community worldview.
The paragraph ends mid-sentence; complete the thought and link the statistic back to TOK concepts like power over knowledge.
The concluding claim restates the prompt but remains generic; strengthen it by referencing each objectâs contribution.
The synthesis of examples is underdeveloped. Explicitly compare how each object illustrates knowledge restriction.
The bibliography header is clear and properly marked, aiding navigation.
The BBC citation for the song is present but missing italics and consistent formatting; standardize entries.
This source relies on a popular music blog; consider adding more scholarly sources to support the TOK analysis.
A secondary blog entry is used for lyric meaning; prioritizing peer-reviewed or primary sources would strengthen the argument.
The duplicate BBC link in the bibliography should be consolidated to avoid redundancy.
The Official Church of Scientology site is cited; ensure formatting of entry matches the rest of the bibliography.
Consider adding IPHR and other human-rights reports earlier in the analysis rather than only in the bibliography, to support the Turkmenistan example.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
